description: This figure presents two dark-field TEM images with corresponding SAED patterns (insets) comparing κ-carbide precipitation in Fe-30Mn-9Al-1Si-0.9C-0.5Mo steels with different phosphorus contents after aging for 0.5 h at 570°C. Panel (a) shows the 0.001% P steel, exhibiting a high number density of very small ordered domains only a few unit cells in dimension, with diffraction intensity corresponding to the perovskite crystal structure of κ-carbide; the scale bar is 10 nm. Panel (b) shows the 0.043% P steel, where the ordered domains have coarsened to an average size of 5 nm and exhibit preferential alignment along <100>; the scale bar is 25 nm. The visual comparison demonstrates that increasing phosphorus significantly increases both the size and number density of κ-carbides. These micrographs support the core conclusion that phosphorus accelerates spinodal decomposition and the ordering reaction during aging, promoting κ-carbide precipitation, yet caption states that phosphorus is randomly distributed between the austenite matrix and κ-carbide rather than partitioning via long-range diffusion.
